verdicts.missing: Profuse or exceptional degree is missing; the AST carries only ordinary abundance.
verdicts.reason: “There is much/many X” gives the right abundance genus but does not distinguish wealth from mere plenty.

verdicts.missing: Abundance of X is replaced by abundance of kinds of X.
verdicts.reason: The AST expresses extensive variety rather than a profuse amount of the referenced thing.

verdicts.missing: Profuse abundance is replaced by a malformed classification of X as a kind of many very big things.
verdicts.reason: It neither asserts that much X exists nor coherently renders abundance as a quality.

verdicts.missing: Profuse abundance is replaced by localized quantity plus an unwarranted denial of variety elsewhere.
verdicts.reason: The spatial concentration and exclusion clauses are not part of this sense of wealth.

verdicts.missing: Profuse abundance is not compositionally predicated; an unattached MORE and an unwarranted public judgment are carried instead.
verdicts.reason: Although it mentions much X, the attempted intensification is unanchored and the added belief about X being a big kind is erroneous.
